defaults.renderer_scope: Health-only stub unless a consumer appears
defaults.advanced_clickhouse: `s3()` ingestion for staged bulk dumps and HTTP async_insert telemetry writes are implemented; projections, skip indexes, S3Queue, url(), and file() remain roadmap

claims.details: `cmd/api/main.go` wires `newAPIMux` with GET handlers for every `/v1/*` route and never registers POST/PUT/DELETE counterparts.

`cmd/control-plane/main.go` exposes the `run-once --job` CLI, `runOnceUsage()` lists the registered jobs, and the long-running service only ticks every 30s, so orchestration happens through the controlled CLI surface.
`cmd/worker-fetch/main.go` contains the fetch node with `fetch-once`/`replay-once` commands and config for ClickHouse/MinIO, demonstrating the crawler surface is wired.
`cmd/worker-parse/main.go` exposes `parse` and `list-parsers` commands backed by the parser registry, showing the parser worker is wired.
`cmd/renderer/main.go` only serves `/health` and listens on :8090, so the renderer binary exists but is a health stub until a consumer appears.
`cmd/bootstrap/main.go` applies migrations, seeds registries, and emits readiness markers as part of the idempotent bootstrap contract.
`docker-compose.yml` brings up ClickHouse, MinIO, bootstrap, API, control-plane, fetch, parse, and renderer services with pinned images.

claims.claim: Compose local HTTP fixture service for E2E
claims.details: `docker-compose.yml` defines `http-fixture` backed by static files under `testdata/http-fixture`, providing deterministic local payloads for E2E checks.
`migrations/clickhouse/0001_init.sql` creates the five logical databases that form the declared zone structure.
`migrations/clickhouse/0002_core_tables.sql` defines `meta.source_registry` and the bootstrap seed ensures the table exists for governance data.
`internal/migrate/http_runner.go` creates `meta.schema_migrations` via an HTTP runner and the bootstrap ensures only this path owns the ledger.
`migrations/clickhouse/0005_baseline_tables.sql` defines `silver.dim_place`, `silver.place_polygon` and `silver.place_hierarchy`, showing the place graph schema is present.

`migrations/clickhouse/0005_baseline_tables.sql` creates `silver.fact_observation`, `silver.fact_event`, `silver.fact_track_point`, `silver.fact_track_segment`, and relation bridges that satisfy the fact claim.

`migrations/clickhouse/0005_baseline_tables.sql` wires `silver.metric_contribution`, `gold.metric_state`, `gold.metric_snapshot`, and `internal/metrics/registry.go` builds the registry records that populate `meta.metric_registry`.

claims.claim: Advanced ClickHouse features (projections, skip indexes, S3Queue, url(), file())
claims.details: Projections, skip indexes, S3Queue, `url()`, and `file()` table-function usage remain deferred; staged `s3()` ingestion and async-insert telemetry have their own implementation claims.

claims.claim: Bulk staged datasets ingest via ClickHouse `s3()` table function
claims.details: `cmd/control-plane/jobs_bulk_dump.go` loads `seed/staged/bulk_dump.csv` from `stage/bulk_dump.csv` into `ops.bulk_dump`, and the table is defined in `migrations/clickhouse/0011_bulk_stage_dataset.sql` while bootstrap registers the stage asset.
`cmd/worker-fetch/main.go` now writes `ops.fetch_log` with `SETTINGS async_insert=1, wait_for_async_insert=1`, letting ClickHouse buffer telemetry while keeping the HTTP insert contract intact.
`cmd/api/main.go` registers these GET handlers with `respond` wrappers that follow the declared envelope, so the health/control surface is active.
`cmd/api/main.go` and `cmd/api/handlers.go` define `jobResource` backed by `gold.api_v1_jobs`, so GET job telemetry endpoints are live.
`cmd/api/main.go` and `cmd/api/handlers.go` map `sourceResource` to `gold.api_v1_sources`, delivering the claimed source metadata.
Place resources in `cmd/api/handlers.go` query `gold.api_v1_places` and support the children/metrics variants, matching the manifest contracts.
`cmd/api/main.go` and the resource specs in `cmd/api/handlers.go` route event/entity/observation queries to the `gold.api_v1_*` views referenced by the manifest.
`cmd/api/main.go` registers `/v1/metrics`, `/v1/analytics/rollups`, and `/v1/analytics/time-series`, all serviced by resource specs and ClickHouse queries.
`cmd/api/main.go` exposes `/v1/analytics/hotspots` and `/v1/analytics/cross-domain`; each route uses `server.listHandler` so the analytics contract exists even if underlying tables are thin.
`cmd/api/main.go` registers `/v1/search`, `/v1/search/places`, and `/v1/search/entities` via `server.combinedSearchHandler` and `searchPlaceResource`, covering the search surface.

Task 5 added all 10 missing core metrics. `internal/metrics/registry.go` now defines all 18 core metrics including this one.

`internal/packs/geopolitical/geopolitical.go` defines all eight geopolitical manifest metrics, including sanction, humanitarian, media acceleration, and infrastructure disruption signals, with registry inserts and contributions.

`internal/packs/maritime/metrics.go` now registers and computes the requested maritime metric family, normalizing the AIS darkness metric to `ais_dark_hours_sum` and adding route, port, anchorage, and flag mismatch signals.

`internal/packs/aviation/types.go` and `internal/packs/aviation/analyze.go` register and emit all eight requested aviation metric IDs, including transponder, altitude, squawk, hold, diversion, and military proximity metrics.

claims.claim: Space metrics (manifest IDs versus implemented names)
claims.details: `internal/packs/space/analysis.go` emits `overpass_density` and `conjunction_risk` rather than the manifest `_score` IDs, so the space pack needs renaming/expansion.
`internal/packs/safety/safety.go` only registers `fire_hotspot` and `sanctions_exposure`, leaving the other safety/security IDs absent.
`cmd/api/main.go` only wires GET handlers for `/v1/jobs`; no POST route exists, so the HTTP job surface remains on the roadmap.
`cmd/control-plane/main.go` documents the run-once CLI contract and exits after valid job execution, so this CLI is the canonical orchestration path.

`cmd/control-plane/jobs_geopolitical.go`, `jobs_maritime.go`, `jobs_aviation.go`, `jobs_space.go`, and `jobs_safety.go` register one run-once ingest job per domain pack, while `place-build` and `promote` cover the shared pipeline jobs.
The migration ledger is created in `internal/migrate/http_runner.go` and `migrations/clickhouse` contains no DDL for `meta.schema_migrations`, so SQL migrations do not own it.
The HTTP runner DDL only defines `version`, `applied_at`, `checksum`, `success`, and `notes`, so the manifest's `id` column claim lacks runtime evidence.
`HTTPRunner.IsApplied` and `Record` use the `checksum` column and `success` flag, so checksum-aware verification is active.
`README.md` and the plans repeatedly describe a single-node baseline with optional cluster add-ons, so single-node mode is the declared default.
